blurb: Fat, rounded leaves glazed in a pearly amethyst-and-pink farina, packed into chunky rosettes on thick stems. Looks like a little pile of smooth lavender river stones.
size: Chunky rosettes ~2–3 in on thick stems

care.light: Bright light with a few hours of gentle sun deepens the amethyst-pink; in low light it greens and the plump leaves space out.
care.water: Soak-and-dry — a deep drink once the leaves soften slightly, then fully dry. Water the soil, never over the delicate bloom.
care.soil: A gritty, fast-draining mix; those swollen leaves rot in anything soggy.
care.temperature: Room temperature; keep above ~30°F and bring in before frost.
care.dormancy: No strong dormancy; it slows in the darkest, coldest weeks — keep it drier then.
care.feeding: A quarter-strength feed once or twice in the growing season.
care.potting: Repot every couple of years; handle by the stem to protect the farina.
care.propagation: Easy from plump leaves and stem cuttings laid on dry mix.
care.bloom: Star-shaped orange-and-red flowers on short stalks in spring.
care.troubles: Overwatering (translucent, mushy leaves) is the main risk; mealybugs between the tight leaves.
